lens: integration QA: audit pre-R6 fix-pass against R4b findings; fresh-eyes on final assembled Phase-1 artifact
headline: All R4b criticals (r4b-qa-1 scaffolder, r4b-qa-2 grant-backed caps) genuinely closed. r4b-qa-3 createdAt triple-stamp closed. r4b-qa-5 canned mermaid closed with real renderer. r4b-qa-6 post:list-through-View-3 closed. 10 new napi Vitest tests all exercise real behaviour (with 1 honest it.skip for Phase-2 view-read). Two R4b majors remain tracked-not-closed-in-code but now honestly disclosed: r4b-qa-4 (exit-4 topo-order still vacuous; doc-caveated) and r4b-qa-8 (PrimitiveHost fallback branch still silent-bypass). Ignored-test markers have STALE reason text on 6 tests — the policy IS now wired, the true Phase-2 blocker is different. No regressions, no vacuous passes introduced.

evidence: crates/benten-caps/src/grant_backed.rs:1-188 — GrantBackedPolicy + GrantReader trait, scope derivation store:<label>:write, read-leg honours compromise #2
crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:1829-1843 — builder.assemble() selects GrantBackedPolicy with BackendGrantReader when use_grant_backed flag set
crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:1680-1697 — .capability_policy_grant_backed() + .with_policy_allowing_revocation() flip the flag
exit_3_cap_denial_routes_on_denied test: grant → call succeeds → revoke → call surfaces ON_DENIED/E_CAP_DENIED (passes)
caps_crud::revoked_cap_routes_to_on_denied test: parallel shape, passes
grant_backed_policy_end_to_end.caveat: Actor is not threaded through WriteContext (policy treats ANY unrevoked grant for scope as sufficient). For per-actor enforcement wait Phase 3 benten-id wiring. Scope derivation is label-based only — per-primitive `requires` property honoured only when call passes a principal. Both are documented in grant_backed.rs:6-28.

evidence: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:2237-2298 — resolve_list_via_view_or_backend: probes content_listing_<label> then content_listing subscriber.read_view, materializes Nodes from returned CIDs in view's sort order, backend fallback only if view absent/empty/errored
crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:2244-2273 — view-first path uses benten_ivm::ViewResult::Cids and preserves view sort order (createdAt asc + disambiguator)
crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:576-592 — register_crud auto-registers a ContentListingView for each non-'post' label so crud('article'):list works out of the box
content_listing_all_returned_after_three_writes + ivm_ten_writes_reflected_in_list_in_order tests pass
post_list_through_view_3.caveat: The view's .read_view() still returns ViewResult::Cids (not rich Nodes) and engine materializes them with an extra backend fetch per item. Phase-1-scoped; fine.

evidence: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:717-754 — handler_to_mermaid reconstructs Subgraph (from SubgraphSpec for DSL, or subgraph_for_crud for crud path) then delegates to benten_eval::diag::mermaid::render
crates/benten-eval/src/diag/mermaid.rs:21-50 — real renderer walks Subgraph.nodes + Subgraph.edges, emits flowchart TD with `nodeId("KIND: label")` per node and `from -->|edge-label| to` per edge
exit_5_mermaid_output_parses_minimal_shape passes with direction-header assertion + edge-arrow check + primitive-kind-substring check
handler_to_mermaid_real_render.caveat: Only delegates through the crud create-shape for zero-config handlers (handler_to_mermaid line 746). If a consumer registers `crud('article')` and calls handler_to_mermaid, it renders the create-shape subgraph. Op-specific rendering (list, get, update, delete) is trivially deferable.

evidence: tools/create-benten-app/template/package.json:13 — `"@benten/engine": "{{bentenEngineSpec}}"` (template token, not wildcard)

tools/create-benten-app/bin.mjs:44-56 — resolveBentenEngineSpec probes packages/engine/package.json; emits `file:<absolute>` when inside monorepo, `^0.1.0` for external users (with TODO phase-2-npm-publish)
scaffolder.test.ts passes — npx create-benten-app, npm install, npm test all run to completion inside the test's tmpdir (observed: 4.8s run, 6/6 smoke assertions green)
scaffolder_npm_install_closure.caveat: The external-user `^0.1.0` fallback path is untested because the engine is unpublished. Phase-2 follow-on is documented with a TODO. Acceptable Phase-1 disposition.

evidence: packages/engine/src/engine.ts:243-251 — registration-time injector removed; comment explicitly cites r4b-qa-3 and names call-time-stamp as the single source of truth
Rust `subgraph_for_crud` stamp documented as defense-in-depth fallback only
createdAt_triple_stamp.caveat: No explicit regression test pins 'stamped exactly once regardless of layer'. exit_2 tests strict-monotonic + re-read-same-value which indirectly guards it, but a dedicated assertion on 'call-time-stamp wins when Rust would also stamp' is not present.

evidence: bindings/napi/index.test.ts:132-343 — new 'napi engine — extended surface' describe block
grantCapability: creates system:CapabilityGrant Node with scope + revoked=false, fetches by CID (line 145-158) — REAL
revokeCapability: before/after countNodesWithLabel delta (line 160-175) — REAL
readView: asserts Phase-1 empty-list-with-ok-true shape with doc comment explaining phase-2 will flip (line 177-195) — CORRECT for phase-1 contract
readView populated: it.skip with TODO(phase-2-view-read) (line 197-213) — HONEST
callAs: dispatches + verifies created CID round-trips via getNode (line 215-229) — REAL
createView: ivmSubscriberCount before+1 delta + system:IVMView label check (line 231-245) — REAL
emitEvent: expects E_PRIMITIVE_NOT_IMPLEMENTED typed error (line 247-255) — REAL negative test
countNodesWithLabel: cross-label isolation check (line 257-267) — REAL
ivmSubscriberCount: pins Phase-1 value of 1 with Phase-2 flip comment (line 269-279) — REAL
openWithPolicy(NoAuth): createNode round-trip (line 281-297) — REAL
openWithPolicy(Ucan): handler registration + call surfaces E_CAP_NOT_IMPLEMENTED or similar (line 299-330) — REAL
deleteEdge: edge created, getEdge returns null after delete, edgesFrom no longer surfaces it (line 332-342) — REAL
napi_ten_previously_untested_methods.caveat: readView Phase-1 empty-list assertion could be strengthened once the engine's read_view returns real data (flip the expect(length).toBe(0) to toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1) and drop the it.skip partner). That's a Phase-2 task, not a Phase-1 gap.

genuinely_phase_2.reason: rebuild() is Phase-1 clear-to-Fresh (no event-log source-of-truth); Phase-2 event-log replay lands later. TRUE Phase-2.
Engine::create_anchor / append_version / read_current_version are Phase-2 anchor-lifecycle APIs. TRUE Phase-2.
Cross-crate wire-through of actor_cid/handler_cid/capability_grant_cid to ChangeEvent emit site. TRUE Phase-2.
HLC (uhlc crate) is Phase-3 scope. TRUE Phase-3.
10k-iteration fuzz harness runs via --ignored in a dedicated CI lane per T12 spec. Correct ignore-shape.

stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.current_reason: capability_policy_grant_backed() builder hook is a Phase-1 no-op
stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.truth_post_fc3f0d1: Policy IS now wired; true blocker is call_with_revocation_at still passes through without scheduling revocation (lib.rs:646-656 is a stub) and ITERATE-batch-boundary cap refresh not yet implemented. Test correctly remains ignored but REASON TEXT IS STALE.

stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.truth: Same as above — stale reason; true blocker is call_with_revocation_at stub + batch-boundary refresh.

Policy IS wired now; register_crud_with_grants at lib.rs:606-608 still aliases to register_crud (documented as phase-2 TODO). The compromise #2 DOC is present in SECURITY-POSTURE.md. The missing piece is routing a targeted read through the policy's check_read at CRUD:get dispatch site. Policy method exists (grant_backed.rs:167-187); the evaluator/dispatch path to invoke it on CRUD:get doesn't.

stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.current_reason: GrantBackedPolicy reads CapabilityGrant nodes from the graph and enforces
stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.truth: Policy DOES read CapabilityGrant nodes now (it's literally what grant_backed.rs:104-165 does). True blocker is schedule_revocation_at_iteration helper is Phase-2 NotImplemented + iterate_write_handler populated helper missing.

Policy exists; per-primitive `requires` property check at WRITE/READ emit time (tied to invariant 13 per-op enforcement) is indeed Phase-2. Reason text is partially correct — the 'GrantBackedPolicy lands Phase-2' part is wrong now, but 'per-primitive requires check lands Phase-2' is right.

stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.current_reason: UcanBackend error routing through Evaluator depends on Phase-2 UcanBackend wire-through
stale_ignore_reason_but_correct_outcome.truth: UcanBackend IS wire-throughable via openWithPolicy('Ucan') and napi test line 299-330 DOES exercise it successfully (surfaces E_CAP_NOT_IMPLEMENTED). The Rust-side test's specific assertion — the routed-through-edge('ON_ERROR')-not-ON_DENIED shape — depends on Evaluator's error-classification pass which needs UcanError → EvalError mapping at the policy boundary. REASON TEXT STALE BUT TEST CORRECTLY IGNORED.

audit_of_19_ignored_tests.recommended_disposition: Update ignore-reason text on 10 tests above to name the TRUE Phase-2 blocker (call_with_revocation_at stub, per-primitive requires check, batch-boundary cap refresh, UcanError routing). Un-ignoring them is not safe — they genuinely need those Phase-2 surfaces. But the stale 'policy is no-op' language misleads future triage.

findings.claim: 10 `#[ignore]` markers carry stale reason text. Post-fc3f0d1 the GrantBackedPolicy IS wired and reads CapabilityGrant Nodes. The true Phase-2 blockers are different: `call_with_revocation_at` is a stub, per-primitive `requires` property check is not wired, ITERATE batch-boundary cap-refresh is unimplemented, UcanError→EvalError classification isn't routed. Ignores are correct; text misleads future readers.
findings.detail: grant_backed.rs:104-165 implements the full GrantBackedPolicy::check_write + check_read flow against any Arc<dyn GrantReader>. assemble() at lib.rs:1834-1837 wires it. The ignore-reasons are leftover from pre-fc3f0d1 triage and will mislead the Phase-2 agent into un-ignoring them prematurely, producing confusing test failures.
findings.suggested_fix: Mechanical sed-pass over the 10 ignore strings to replace 'GrantBackedPolicy reads CapabilityGrant nodes from the graph and enforces; blocked on...' with the accurate blocker. Proposed per-test wording in the audit_of_19_ignored_tests section above.

findings.claim: r4b-qa-8 remains open: the no-active-frame fallback branch still writes directly to the backend bypassing the capability hook. Defensive scoping (no test calls it) but the silent-bypass shape persists.
findings.detail: Lines 2001-2008 (put_node), 2023-2028 (put_edge), 2040-2043 (delete_node), 2054-2058 (delete_edge) all contain `drop(guard); self.backend.put_node(...)` paths when active_call is empty. No policy.check_write, no ChangeEvent attribution. Current test suite never triggers these (impl PrimitiveHost is called only from Evaluator which is called only from dispatch_call which always pushes a frame) — so the bypass is dormant. But a future refactor that invokes `<Engine as PrimitiveHost>::put_node` directly (e.g., a benchmark, a test helper, a sync-import path) will silently skip capability enforcement.
findings.suggested_fix: Make the fallback branch `Err(EvalError::Backend("impl PrimitiveHost called outside a dispatch_call frame"))`. If dormant, hard-failing is safer than silently bypassing. Add a `<Engine as PrimitiveHost>::put_node` direct-call test asserting the error. 1-hour task.

findings.claim: r4b-qa-4 downgrade-documentation landed but the test is still vacuous on topological ordering. HandlerPredecessors::predecessors_of always returns `&[]`, so the inner assertion never fires regardless of step order.
findings.detail: fc3f0d1/c9670f8 added the Phase-1-caveat doc (exit_criteria_all_six.rs:197-204) and tightened the step-count to `>=2`. The topo-order loop at lines 250-260 is still a no-op as documented. exit_4 therefore validates (a) trace produces >=2 steps and (b) each step has duration_us > 0. It does NOT validate topological order. Phase-2 target: populate HandlerPredecessors from stored SubgraphSpec and generate trace steps with per-primitive distinct node_cids.
findings.suggested_fix: No action for Phase 1 — the R4b disposition to doc-caveat rather than un-scope is fine. Phase 2 ticket: 'exit-4 topo-order becomes enforcing' with the HandlerPredecessors fill-in as the sole blocker.

findings.claim: r4b-qa-9 only partially closed. Compromise #2 section was appended (c9670f8), but #1, #3, #4, #5, #7, and #8 still have no section. #8 was explicitly closed at 25d2893 per commit message but SECURITY-POSTURE.md does not record the closure.
findings.detail: Current SECURITY-POSTURE.md §'Named Compromises' has subsections for #6 and #2 only. CLAUDE.md references 7 compromises (compromise 1-5 + 7-8), of which only 2 and 6 land in the doc. Compromise #3 (ErrorCode enum in benten-core) is low-risk and tested by compromises_regression.rs:132-141 but undocumented. Compromise #5 (no write rate limits) has a metric-regression test but no documentation. Compromise #7 ([[bin]] gating) has a regression test but no doc.
findings.suggested_fix: Append 6 stub sections (1/3/4/5/7/8) to SECURITY-POSTURE.md §'Named Compromises', each 3-5 lines: what, why, Phase-2 closure plan, current pin-test location. Compromise #8 gets a 'CLOSED at fc3f0d1' note (Engine::call now dispatches through Evaluator). 30-min documentation-only task. Low severity — the regression tests hold the contract; the doc just records the narrative.

findings.claim: Two `register_crud_with_grants` / `call_with_revocation_at` builder-shape APIs are Phase-1 passthrough stubs. They exist so tests that spell the grant-backed intent compile, but they deliver no behavioural difference over `register_crud` / `call_as`.
findings.detail: register_crud_with_grants at lib.rs:606 calls self.register_crud(label). call_with_revocation_at at lib.rs:646 calls self.dispatch_call(handler_id, op, input, Some(actor.clone())) — the `_scope` and `_n` parameters are unused. Both are marked with TODO(phase-2-grant-backed-policy). The current Phase-1 GrantBackedPolicy DOES enforce on regular register_crud + call paths, so the stubs' Phase-2 delta is narrower than documented: the real Phase-2 change is (a) routing the `scope` arg through the registration metadata so per-handler scope overrides work, and (b) the `_n` scheduling hook for mid-iteration revocation tests.
findings.suggested_fix: Document the narrow Phase-2 delta in the rustdoc — they're not entirely phase-2-shaped stubs, they're 'identical to register_crud/call_as in Phase 1; Phase 2 adds per-handler scope override + mid-call revocation scheduling'. No behaviour change needed.

findings.claim: The readView test asserts Phase-1 empty-list behaviour but uses a positive assertion (`outcome.list.length).toBe(0)`) which will silently continue to pass if Phase-2 fills in the payload. The paired it.skip at line 197-213 is the Phase-2-enabling test; coupling is weak.
findings.detail: When Phase-2 lands the view-read materialization, the Phase-1 assertion `expect(outcome.list.length).toBe(0)` will START FAILING and force a test update. That's the correct direction — but an ergonomic future-dev would prefer `expect(outcome.list.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)` with a comment and the paired it.skip carrying the real shape-check. Current arrangement works but requires the Phase-2 agent to remember to drop the length===0 line AND unskip the follow-up. Slightly fragile.
findings.suggested_fix: Swap the length===0 assertion to length>=0 with a comment pointing at the it.skip. When Phase-2 lands, the it.skip flips to it and the empty-list line is deleted. Smaller context-switch for the Phase-2 agent.

test_suite_accuracy_vs_shipping_product: exit_3_cap_denial_routes_on_denied now tests REAL behaviour end-to-end — grant → call succeeds → revoke → call surfaces ON_DENIED. Closes the long-standing 'builder has grant-backed intent but policy is NoAuth' drift.
post:list path now exercises View 3 IVM end-to-end when content_listing view is registered; backend label-index fallback is explicitly documented as the without_ivm / un-registered safety net.

handler_to_mermaid renders the REAL registered subgraph via benten_eval::diag::mermaid::render — no more canned 3-node flowchart; exit_5 and packages/engine mermaid.test.ts both exercise the real renderer.

10 new napi Vitest tests exercise real behaviour with observable mutations (Node writes, CID round-trips, count deltas, typed-error throws). One it.skip is HONESTLY marked for a Phase-2 read-view surface — no vacuous passes introduced.

19 ignored tests: 9 are genuine Phase-2/Phase-3 (version chain, ivm event-log replay, attribution wire-through, HLC, fuzz harness). 10 are STALE-reason ignores where policy is actually wired but the test needs additional Phase-2 surfaces (call_with_revocation_at stub, per-primitive requires, batch-boundary refresh, UcanError routing). ALL 19 are legitimately deferred — none are masking R5 gaps.

exit_4 topo-order loop remains vacuous (HandlerPredecessors returns empty slice) but the surrounding test has been honestly downgraded in its doc to name what it actually asserts (step-count + per-step timing). Fine for Phase 1.
r4b-qa-8 PrimitiveHost fallback branch: dormant but silently bypasses capability. No test exercises it; future refactor could awaken it. Minor risk.

gaps_that_remain.area: Stale ignore-reason text on 10 grant-backed-related tests
PrimitiveHost fallback branch silently bypasses capability check
HandlerPredecessors stub + exit_4 topo-order loop vacuous
register_crud_with_grants / call_with_revocation_at are documented as Phase-2 stubs but the Phase-2 delta is narrower than documented
readView Phase-1 empty-list assertion shape is future-fragile

what_i_ignored: Rust unit-test quality breakdown (owned by rust-test-reviewer R6 pass)
Coverage completeness vs R2 landscape (owned by rust-test-coverage R6 pass)
Rust lint / clippy / fmt gate (separate CI lanes, currently green)
Rust crate architecture / layering (owned by architect-reviewer)
Named-compromise individual correctness (owned by security-auditor / engine-philosophy)
top_3_integration_concerns_post_r6: Stale ignore-reason text on 10 tests — low-effort fix, moderate-impact prevention of Phase-2 triage churn.
PrimitiveHost fallback branch is dormant silent-bypass — keep as-is if Phase 1 wants stability; hard-fail if Phase 1 wants defensive correctness. Low risk either way given current call-sites.

The Phase-1/Phase-2 boundary on capability enforcement is NOW correctly located — policy IS wired, REAL tests pass. Remaining gaps (per-primitive requires, call_with_revocation_at, batch-boundary refresh) are honestly deferred. The pre-R4b 'grant-backed as unlanded stub' concern is GENUINELY resolved, not doc-papered.
confidence: high — every closure verified by reading the actual source + running the actual test suite. 466 Rust / 10 TS / 18 napi / 8 scaffolder all pass; 19 ignores reviewed individually; stale reason text identified by cross-referencing the current grant_backed.rs impl against the ignore-string claims.
recommended_disposition: PASS for R6. Ship Phase 1 on this commit (fc3f0d1) with the 6 followups above as Phase-1-polish or Phase-2-ticket material. The three R4b criticals are genuinely closed; the test suite accurately reflects the shipping product's behaviour; no vacuous passes were introduced; the ignored tests are honestly scope-deferred. Phase 1 meets its exit criteria.
notes: Phase 1 assembled a real end-to-end artifact: content-addressed graph with deterministic CIDs, CRUD dispatch through an evaluator that honours a grant-backed capability policy, IVM View 3 materializing content listings, a scaffolder whose generated project installs + tests green, and a mermaid-visualization surface over the registered subgraph structure. The six exit criteria are exercised by REAL assertions, not substring-sniff passes. The remaining doc/hygiene gaps are all low-severity and disjoint from the shipping-product contract. Fresh-eyes verdict: Phase 1 is ready.
